reviews.review_title: I wish I could understand it
reviews.review_description: I just got the game and played the tutorial but after it has me place the barrels full of water on the shelves the instructions show the step after that a nozzle should be there and I looked around and found no nozzle and it left me very confused. I looked at walkthroughs and found no help just some weird guy talking in a wizard voice.

For the people wanting a short review, this game is okay, at least. It's great for a simulator and fits its role well. But I think the price is a bit steep for 17.99$, It's quite glitchy and gets VERY boring after the 3 hour mark. 


Now, for the people wanting a long review, this game is still okay, at least. When I say it's boring after the 3 hour mark, I was trying to accommodate everyone else playing this game. For me? It got boring after 30 minutes. I got this game for 10 dollars, and I think that is the perfect amount that one should pay for this game, not 17.99$. Now, I understand this game is very good with what it actually is. If you want to feel like you are running a inn? Well, you have 100% got one. I can tell a lot of love went into this game, but I'm sorry but this is a review and I have to be honest with you, and my honesty is that this game is a drought out, overly expensive, glitchy mess of a game. I have tons of other stuff to yap about such a tunneling, the 4 year early access as of 2026, the bleak updates, and the AI art. But I think I should end it here, I don't wanna spend 5 hours writing a review. Thanks for reading, and have a good day. :)

The cooking and brewing is very immersive and fun! I like how people can steal, fight, and yell, it makes the tavern life even more realistic. One thing I think would be cool is multiplayer. You could have a friend be a bodyguard if your to broke to hire one! Also, it would be cool if you were able to fight the drunken diners by punching them and then you could throw them out when they’re knocked out.

This game is pretty fun. Being able to cook and serve beverages and soups is pretty cool and satisfying. 

Some ideas maybe for the next update could be…

-Customers ordering there own foods (only the meals that you have made)
-some more maps with more rooms such as maybe outdoor tables or rooms, stables maybe
-MULTPLAYER!!
-Utensils like spoons for the soups (must be bought in a store)
-limited cups and bowls (Also must pay in a store)
-more factions like noblemen, lords, knights,etc
-Possibly different types of foods like breads as appetizers.

Just a disclaimer that these ideas are just suggestions, devs you don’t have to do these ideas but it would be cool to see.

This game has so much potential and is already good even in early access. Definitely a solid 8.7/10. Thanks for this awesome game. Definitely a new experience in vr

reviews.review_title: Please go back to making this game
reviews.review_description: Back when I played this it was so good and calming it would be better if it had more to it

reviews.review_title: Very fun but I broke the cleaning guy
reviews.review_description:
Great game, very addictive. The motion sickness controls are very important (darkening the edges of the screen when you walk) so definitely use those features..  unfortunately you cannot teleport with items like the broom yet so you'll need to manage your motion sickness when doing that. I upgraded the cleaning guy and now he stands outside dissociating really hard while I do all his cleaning for him. It's so tedious doing the cleaning so I won't be playing the game again until it's fixed. 

For anyone trying to serve stew (it took me a while) be aware you need to keep throwing logs under the stove with a 10+ second gap until it grows large enough to actually cook (might need 2-3 logs!). Thankfully your freshly cut logs teleport inside so you don't need to carry it all
I came back to play the game and all my progressed was gone everything I worked so hard for that just for it to be deleted

I absolutely love this game! I particularly enjoy interactive, hands-on experiences, which is why I was excited to dive in. However, I found that the tutorial could benefit from some significant updates. I spent quite a bit of time stuck on the first part involving the water and the sprout, specifically at step 4 of the tutorial. It was a bit frustrating, but once I figured it out, I became completely hooked! Now, I'm eagerly looking forward to future updates and new content that will enhance the gameplay even further.

Honestly I've seen people play a tutorial on YouTube over a year ago, now there is just a campaign or sandbox mode?? 
Seems to be going backwards. Tutorial is atrocious. 

Anyways the mechanics are ok, I really enjoyed running a tavern in medieval dynasty so thought I would try this out.

It has the strangest crouch function I have ever seen however it is useful for seated players! Being able to warp up and down 😂 else I'd never reach stuff. 

I can see it's going to get stressful trying to cook clean chop replenish drinks and serve all at once. As well as collecting payments and throwing trouble makers out, so I'm trying to unlock the staff see how helpful it is. 

Only thing about the graphics is the customers, the faces are scary.

Everything else looks good. I Like that you can decorate as well.. Don't like that the backpack doesn't go on your back all the time, or that thr hammer got stuck to my back with the back pack in my hand?? Small bugs to work out I think. 

I also like there's other areas to unlock.

It's a small studio producing the game, I don't know why others are so hung up on using a little AI to help with the tutorial art or whatever, what does it matter? Doesn't make the game any less of anything. 

So I couldn't place a drink, by trying again I grabbed the customer by mistake and my reputation went down.. That is very disappointing!

Please paste the discord link I can't find it. Thank you
Really fun but I did not know how to get ingredients until my friend told me

I love this game! I found myself living my medieval tavern dream, it’s very addictive!
I just beg the developers to make the body slots moveable, as a disabled player who plays mostly laying down this game is super accessible for me in so far every aspect EXCEPT the cleaning cloth, I have taken to cleaning the tables with the mop which works but isn’t ideal having to run back and forth to the kitchen for every minor spillage.

reviews.review_title: I’m genuinely in love with the game
reviews.review_description:
The way the game is set up is AMAZING I love the way you can make your own lager and ale and do not get me started on the cooking I love it, it’s such a cosy game. I’ve only been playing for about a day and omg I love it the concept is beautiful.

Ps. I’ve named that cleaner Micah.

Lost all respect for this game when i realized that ai was used for the tutorial images, will not give this game another chance unless the ai is removed. hire a real artist, heck even just scribbling randomly for the tutorial art would be better; maybe even more clear.

The game overall is amazing and very well. There is a bug where for some reason NPCs will get randomly stuck in a fighting motion forever until you throw them out. I also have no idea on how to buy rooms because I turned tutorial off in beginning and when I turn it back on it's completely reseted

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the feedback, really glad you’re enjoying the game! And thanks for reporting the bug too. We’ll do our best to fix the NPCs getting stuck in the fighting animation in a future update.

About the rooms: they unlock in the second (larger) tavern as you increase that tavern’s reputation. As you level up its reputation, you’ll get rewards tied to that specific tavern, including rooms.

reviews.review_description: This game is incredible it will completely Assemble the medieval era of time, and you have your own little supermarket. You can cook like Gordan Ramesy and can create your own tavern. YES it deleted your progress but that's a fresh new start. I HIGHLY recommend multiplayer too! This game is beautiful and you will fall in love with it just like i did!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your review, this was an absolute joy to read!

We’re really happy to hear how much you connected with the medieval tavern fantasy and the freedom to cook, build, and run your own place. We also truly appreciate your understanding about the progress reset and seeing it as a fresh start, that means a lot to us, especially after such a big update.

Multiplayer/Co-op is something many players are excited about, and while we can’t promise anything yet, it’s definitely something we’re interested in exploring as the game grows.

Thanks again for the amazing support and recommendation. Messages like this genuinely motivate our small team to keep pushing the game forward!

reviews.review_description: I was really excited to play Innkeeper Vr because the concept looked fun this game is amazing im so glad that i decided to buy it money well spent. all i would say is that there needs to be a better tutorial i would say make players do it immediately so players know what to do either way amazing game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the kind words, we’re really glad you’re enjoying the game! 😊

And thank you for the suggestion about the tutorial as well. We agree that the onboarding for new players can be clearer, and we’re currently working on improving the tutorial and overall early-game guidance so it’s easier to understand what to do at the start.

Feedback like this helps us a lot while we continue developing the game. Thanks again for playing and for supporting the project! 🍻

reviews.review_description: Used ai + couldnt make progress at all after placing down the water barrel, couldn't find the spigot, some instruction missed out on so much

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thanks for your feedback.

Barrels can be placed in two different locations depending on the drink state:
- If the drink is already finished, the barrel should be placed on the bar counter in the blue highlighted slot. When placed there, the barrel automatically gets a tap, allowing you to pour drinks into mugs.
- If it’s an alcoholic drink that still needs fermentation, it must first be placed on the fermentation stand in the kitchen (again, the correct slot highlights when you hold the item). After fermentation is complete, you can carry the barrel to the bar counter to serve it.

In general, when you pick up an object in the game, the correct placement slot is highlighted, showing where that item should go for the current step.

We are continuing to improve the tutorial and instructions so that new players can understand the systems more easily.

Regarding AI: yes, we do use AI tools in development, and we’re transparent about that. In fact, on our Steam store page there is a clear disclosure about AI usage, as required by Steam. The Meta store currently does not have a similar disclosure system yet.

AI tools are widely used across the industry today, and we will continue using them as part of our development process. If the use of AI is something you strongly disagree with, our projects may simply not be the right fit for you - and that’s completely okay.

reviews.review_description: This game is amazing i play this game day and night, its so addictive and fun. But there is one problem with this game and its that whenever a customer comes in they ask for either a bowl or a cup they don't say like "Apple juice" or porridge and its kind of annoying because you just give them something random. It would just be way more fun if it was more specific. Thanks a lot if you did read this and since this game is in early access i wont let this problem affect my rating. I cant wait for future updates.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thank you so much for the kind words and for taking the time to share your thoughts, we're really glad you're enjoying the game! 😊

The reason customers ask for a bowl or a cup instead of a specific dish or drink is intentional. During development we tested more specific orders, but it often created frustrating situations e.g. where players didn’t have the exact item available. Since preparing food and drinks takes time, guests would frequently leave before the order could be completed.

This became especially problematic in the later stages of the game, where a lot is happening at once and the tavern can get quite chaotic. Specific orders added unnecessary pressure and punished players for things that were sometimes outside their control, which made the experience less fun overall.

By keeping requests more flexible, you can focus on managing the tavern, serving guests quickly, and keeping the flow going instead of worrying about missing one exact item.

That said, we really appreciate feedback like yours, it helps us improve the game as development continues. Thanks again for playing! 🍻

reviews.review_description: Please let us stab with knife

reviews.review_title: THRE THINGS TO MAKE IT BETTER
reviews.review_description:
1. More maps mabye like the king's castle

2. More sandbox setings

3. MORE INTERACTION WITH CUSTOMER (violence type)

after i put water Barrel down on the shelf absolutely nothing happens, i cant progress.


Update: im dumb- 

Seriously tho yall gotta make it a bit 
more clear where it goes.

developer_response.body:
Hi! No worries, this part is easy to miss, and we agree it should be clearer.

For the Water barrel, you don’t place it on the kitchen shelf/stand. You need to put it on the bar counter in the main hall. When you’re holding the barrel, the correct placement spot will highlight in blue. Once it’s placed there, the tap appears automatically, and you’ll be able to pour into mugs.

The kitchen stand becomes useful later for fermentation (alcohol barrels), so it can feel misleading early on.

If this fixed it for you, we’d really appreciate it if you could adjust your star rating, it makes a big difference for a small indie team. Thank you!

reviews.review_description:
I really liked the last version of this game, I played a ton of it in fact. With the newest update this December, all of that progress has been lost. Hours wasted so they could update the game to be more or less the exact same with the progression update. If you are a new player, you will probably like this game, but I’m definitely frustrated with how this reset essentially leaves players who have supported this game for a while in the dust. I wish there was either a way to continue to play the old update with my progress intact or that the developers had at least transferred the money I’d earned to the new update if I’m going to have to start over. I am definitely discouraged from starting to play again, despite this being a game I’d loved so much just a few weeks prior. I was really anticipating this update too, and was so disappointed when I returned and saw my money at 0 and my old tavern was gone.

Update: I did in fact play through the new campaign. The changes work for the most part and progression is pretty painless and quite similar to what was established in the last update. I did enjoy my play-through still even though it was more or less the same as the version prior, but I look forward to a potential co-op update. I would still highly recommend this game for those interested, it’s probably one of the most engaging on the platform and I’m excited to see what’s next for this game.

developer_response.body:
Hi there!

First of all, thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts, and we’re really sorry for how frustrating the progress reset felt. We know how much time players put into building their taverns, and losing that progress is never a good feeling.

This reset wasn’t something we wanted to do lightly. The update included a major engine upgrade and a completely new progression system under the hood. We spent a long time trying to migrate old saves to the new system, testing different solutions, but unfortunately none of them worked in a stable and safe way for all players. In the end we had to make the difficult decision to start fresh for everyone.

The reason for this step was to make sure the game can continue to grow on modern tech and allow us to keep adding new features and content in the future.
The good news is that future updates will no longer affect player saves - from now on we’ll only be adding new content without changing existing progression.
We really appreciate that you gave the new campaign a chance and shared such detailed feedback. It means a lot to us that you still enjoyed the experience overall. 

Thank you for supporting the game and for all the time you’ve already spent in Innkeeper VR 💛

reviews.review_description:
This game is beyond addictive and rewarding. I've been having more fun than I thought I was with a game that seemed chaotic and stressful. The progression and the challenge is super rewarding. Some bugs do exist, but it's nothing that makes the game awkward or annoying. Definitely give it a shot, it's totally worth it.

POST MAJOR UPDATE EDIT:
It's still incredible. I see a lot of others were giving poorer ratings since their progress was wiped, and I totally understand the frustration. My progress was also wiped with the new update, however I don't personally see that as a bad thing. I think with the way they've created the progression system, it makes total sense why some saves needed to be wiped. I do enjoy it even still, and I find it fun to rediscover and re-fall-in-love with this amazing game. This time with more things to do!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your kind words!

We’re very grateful for your understanding regarding the progress reset after the major update. That was a tough decision for us, and seeing players like you recognize why it was necessary (and even enjoy rediscovering the game) really means the world to our small team.

We’re glad the new progression gives you more to explore and more reasons to fall back in love with the game. Thank you for sticking with us, for your patience, and for such thoughtful support. We’ll keep doing our best to make Innkeeper VR even better!

reviews.review_title: Amazing game but I lost my progress
reviews.review_description: This game is amazing I have played sense the water moved at 1fps this is an amazing tavern stile game even though the characters are polygon’s. I’ve finished the game I had both taverns with all the rooms and didn’t play for about 2 months when I came back All of my progress was lost I don’t care about my old save data just wanted to let you know

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the 5-star review and for sticking with the game for so long, especially since the very veeery early days. That truly means a lot to us!

You’re right about the lost progress: it was caused by a major engine upgrade and a full progression system rework. Part of why we had to do this was to move the game onto more modern tech so we can properly support it going forward and to seriously explore bigger features players keep asking for, like possible co-op in the future. 

We’re really sorry that your old save was affected, even if you’re okay with it now. The good news is that future updates will no longer reset progress, and we’ve also added Sandbox Mode as a flexible way to jump back in and play freely.

Thank you again for the support and for taking the time to let us know. It’s players like you who motivate our small team to keep improving the game!

reviews.review_description: Best game

I love this game so much, from brewing drinks, cooking food and playing the banjo, it’s so much fun! Although it definitely needs multiplayer. Imagine you and your friends cooking and brewing together. And another addition would be a sandbox mode, where you don’t need to unlock anything, and have an option for sandbox multiplayer. PLEASE READ DEVELOPING TEAM!!!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the feedback, we’re really glad you’re enjoying the game! 😊

Just to let you know, Sandbox mode has already been added, so you can play freely without needing to unlock everything.

As for multiplayer, we hear this request quite often and we understand why players would love to run a tavern together with friends. It’s something we’ve discussed and are considering, but since the game was originally designed as a single-player experience, implementing multiplayer would require very significant changes. Because of that, we can’t promise anything specific right now.

Still, we really appreciate you sharing your ideas and supporting the game. Thanks for playing! 🍻

reviews.review_description: Not too far in the game so far but the game is really fun, there's some bugs but that's expected for a early access. The best part by far has to be the cooking or making drinks, it's satisfying to successfully make a recipe.

We’re really glad you’re enjoying the game so far, especially the cooking and drink-making, that’s one of our favorite parts too. 😊 And yes, since the game is still in early access, we’re continuing to fix bugs and improve things along the way. Thanks for playing and for your support! 🍻

reviews.review_title: Where did all my progress go?
reviews.review_description: I was going to play innkeeper vr but then when I opened it I saw everything was different in the main area and all my progress was gone to my cash to my innkeep I had 3 rooms so many decorations so mutch cash and boom all gone away I know its just a game but I have anxiety so I stressed out and its a fun game but this is really not my favorite I don't like how any of this looks honestly it was a lot more charming also all my Progress is gone and its really upsetting I love this game but right now I can't like this game  I used to have a 4 star review but actually this is to long I think I made my point. I just played a bit wtf is this water also really ai ok now you crossed the line what the hell revert now, revert now come on why do this i am keeping this here until it gose back to normal because every is bad about this update people asking for a update I was fine but this, this is horrible it went from a good game to something that I look down to

developer_response.body:
Hi there,

First of all, we are really sorry for what happened with your progress and for how upsetting this update felt for you. We know how much time, effort, and emotion you put into your progress, and we completely understand how frustrating it is to lose that. 

Your saves won’t be affected by future updates anymore. From now on, we’ll be adding only new content without changing previous mechanics.

For the entire two years that Innkeeper VR has existed, we have always kept players’ saves across updates, exactly because we know how important that progress is. This is not a case where things were just randomly wiped or reset for no reason. This was a forced step, and it was a very hard decision for us too. 

This update includes a huge engine upgrade and a completely new progression system under the hood. That step was critical so we can keep supporting the game on modern tech and so we can at least seriously explore things that many players ask about, like the possibility of co-op in the future.

We spent a lot of time trying different ways to move old saves to the new system, testing multiple approaches, but none of them worked in a way that was stable and safe for everyone. In the end, we had to accept that all players would need to start fresh, and for that we can only offer our sincerest apologies.

We completely understand why you feel so negative about the update right now. Even so, we honestly hope that if you ever decide to give the new version another chance, the new progression and future improvements will feel better over time. Thank you for all the hours you already spent in Innkeeper VR and for being so honest with us. Your feedback really does help us make the game better 💛

reviews.review_description: When I first got this game it was recommended by a friend of mine and I loved it I played daily until I had finished the first tavern. Later on in the year when I saw a new update I was exited but when I joined it turned out that it reset my progress which made me sad. Please fix this!!!

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thank you for sharing your experience, and we’re really sorry this happened, we completely understand why losing your progress felt discouraging.

For nearly two years, Innkeeper VR updates always preserved player saves, because we know how important that progress is. Unfortunately, this particular update was different. It included a major engine upgrade and a complete overhaul of the progression system under the hood. We explored multiple ways to migrate old saves, tested several approaches, and ultimately none of them were stable or safe for all players. Making everyone start fresh was a very difficult decision for us as well, and we truly apologize for the frustration it caused.

The good news is that future updates will not wipe your progress again. From now on, we’re only adding new content without changing existing progression systems.

This situation is also the reason we introduced Sandbox Mode. Sandbox lets you jump back in without pressure: all taverns are unlocked from the start, and you can customize settings to rebuild roughly where you left off or even go beyond that. It’s meant as a safe, stress-free way to enjoy the game again on your own terms.

We genuinely appreciate the time you spent with Innkeeper VR and your honest feedback. If you ever decide to give the game another chance, we hope the new progression and Sandbox mode make the experience much more enjoyable. Thank you for supporting a small indie team, it truly means a lot to us!

I love the music and atmosphere reminds me of slime rancher and botw plus Skyrim which is rlly nostalgic as well as making my bard jam out on his lute while I go hardcore on the cooking fr ! About the repetition: that’s a fair point, and it’s something we’re actively working on improving with new mechanics, progression changes, and additional content.

Thanks for taking the time to share both what you loved and what could be better. Feedback like this helps us shape the future of the game, and we hope you’ll enjoy jumping back in as it continues to grow.

reviews.review_title: Fun game but confusing at times
reviews.review_description: I don't understand how to serve my stew I got cabbage and it gave me instructions, I did that and added the chopped cabbage into the water and I made the stew but I tried using the spoon and then scooping it with the bowl to serve it but nothing was working.. I would like it if the game had more instructions on what to do because I've wasted like 4 soups trying to do this and now I'm broke

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thanks a lot for taking the time to leave a review and explain what happened and sorry for the frustration you experienced. Losing several soups while trying to figure it out definitely isn’t a great experience.

Let’s go through the cooking process step-by-step, because usually the issue happens at one of these stages:
1. Place the pot onto the cooking slot on the stove.
2. Pour water into the pot (until you see 2/2 water).
3. Add 3 ingredients. You’ll see circular progress bars around the ingredient icons above the pot - wait until they fully complete and disappear.
4. After all 3 ingredients are added, you should see a ladle hint. At this point you need to stir the pot with the ladle.
5. After stirring, the soup is ready to cook: you can light the fire in the stove at this stage if it isn’t already lit.
6. The cooking timer will start once the pot is stirred and the stove is burning.
7. When the soup is finished, the pot will display the dish name, price, and portion count (for example 10/10 portions if the water was filled to 2/2).

Only after the soup is fully cooked will you be able to pour it into bowls.

If the pot looks like soup but you can’t scoop it into bowls, it usually means one of the steps above didn’t register.

We’re also working on making the instructions clearer in the game so this process is easier to understand.

If you’d like, feel free to join our Discord - we’re happy to help troubleshoot there and answer any questions: https://discord.com/invite/ZhUQwEearp

Thanks again for playing and for the feedback!

reviews.review_description: This is an amazing game but I leave it a 4 star because I feel it can be improved and given a multiplayer mode . But other than this it’s an amazing game

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your kind words!

We’re really glad you’re enjoying the game. Multiplayer is something we see requested a lot, and it’s definitely on our radar. That said, it’s a complex feature to do right in VR, so while we’re exploring the idea, we can’t make any promises yet.

Thanks again for the support and for sharing your thoughts, feedback like this helps guide where we take the game next!

reviews.review_description: it's a cool game love it and the developers did a great job for such a small team absolute amazing game i don't know why people were giving you one star reviews it's so cool realistic and absolutely amazing . and it makes it 1000 times better because no internet required but i do get it because it's only a one player game but still amazing game the developers did a great job like absolutely amazing job

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the amazing review! We really appreciate the kind words and your support. 😊 It means a lot to our small team to hear that you're enjoying the game and the experience we’re trying to create. Thanks for playing and for taking the time to share your thoughts! 🍻

reviews.review_description: How do I make my cabbage soup

developer_response.body:
Thank you, we’re really glad you’re enjoying the game!

To make cabbage soup:
1. Pour water into the pot
2. Chop and add 3 cabbages
3. Stir with the ladle
4. Put firewood inside the stove to light the fire, then place the pot into the cooking slot above the fire and let it cook

That’s it, once it finishes cooking, the soup is ready. Enjoy running your tavern! 🍲🍻

reviews.review_description: I know why you reset all our progress. Not a big deal. But still, i had 500 coins and a cleand. Great game tho. Also add multiplayer. Maybe like a "if your reindeer has the game you can play for free in co-op" type. Scenario.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for being so understanding about the progress reset, we really appreciate that.

We definitely hear how many players want co-op/multiplayer, and it’s something we’re genuinely interested in exploring. That said, it’s a complex feature, especially in VR, so we can’t promise anything yet. We’ll keep researching and experimenting to see what’s realistically possible in the future.

Thanks again for the support and for sharing your ideas!

reviews.review_description: I had this game for a while but i havent seen any new updates did you creaters abonded us?. Im honstly kinda worred

developer_response.body:
Hey! Thanks so much for sticking with us and for the honest note!

We haven’t abandoned the game at all. We’ve been heads down on a big update. It turned out bigger than we expected, so we took extra time to test and polish everything to make sure nothing gets in the way of your fun. Right now we’re waiting on a couple of small platform approvals, and we’re almost ready to drop it.

We also need a bit more time for optimization. Many players use Quest 2, so we want everything to run smoothly on all devices without performance issues.

Please follow the news in our Discord, we’ll announce there as soon as we have the release dates: https://discord.com/invite/ZhUQwEearp

Thanks again for your patience and for being part of the community! 🙌

reviews.review_description: This game is fantastic! The cooking is fun and throwing people out of the inn is exhilarating! The only thing that would make the game better is to add multiplayer.
The game was pretty fun before this newest update, but now the new update uses ai in the tutorial. Wish I could give it 0 stars

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thanks for taking the time to leave a review and for saying the game was pretty fun! That really matters to us as a small team!

About the “AI in the tutorial” you mentioned: we added an optional tutorial with images that explain the basic mechanics. For some specific elements in those images, our designer used AI as a helper tool to generate a rough base. It was never a “one-click AI art” dropped straight into the game. Our designer spends hours correcting, repainting, and polishing everything to get the final result. The direction, style decisions, and quality control are still entirely in human hands.

We’re a tiny indie studio, and we have to cover pretty much every part of development ourselves. That means we end up doing literally everything, and we just don’t have the budget to hire a dedicated specialist for every area. Even when we do use AI, it doesn’t “make the game for us”, it only helps us speed up certain parts of the work.

The latest update we worked on took more than half a year, and we worked on it every day, trying to make the experience unique and fun for our players.

That’s why it’s a bit disheartening to see a 1★ “don’t buy” verdict for a game you say you enjoyed. Reviews have a huge impact on whether a small indie game can reach new players and whether we can afford to keep updating it instead of walking away.

You’re absolutely entitled to your opinion and your rating, we’re not arguing with that. We just want anyone reading this to have the full picture: the core game you liked is still the same, and AI is used as a helper tool in a few parts of the art pipeline, not as a replacement for people or for game design.

If you still feel a 1★ “don’t buy” is the right message, we’ll accept that. But if this was more of an emotional reaction to the “AI” than to your actual experience with the game, we’d really appreciate it if you’d reconsider your rating or at least your wording.
Either way, thanks again for playing our game!

reviews.review_description: i can not play any more do to the age rating being 16 . (i can lose a game again to age rating not again ) please can you lower it

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for your message and for caring about the game.

Unfortunately we can’t simply lower the age rating. The rating is set automatically by the IARC system based on the answers we give about the game’s content, and stores like the Meta Horizon Store require us to follow that rating. Because the game includes brewing alcohol and serving alcoholic drinks, many regions classify it as suitable only for older players, so we aren’t allowed to just change the number on our side.

The good news is that Meta now lets younger players ask a parent or guardian for access to apps that are rated above their age.

If your parents already bought the game, you can try this:
1. Open the Meta Horizon / Quest store and go to the Innkeeper VR page.
2. Try to download or launch the game.
3. When you see the message that the app is age-restricted, choose the option to send a request to your parent/guardian.
4. Your parent should then get a notification or email in their Meta / Quest app where they can approve access to the game.

As long as your account stays connected to your parent’s account and they approve the request, you should be able to keep playing even though the age rating is 16+. 

We’re really sorry that this change caused trouble for you, and we hope you’ll be able to get access again with your parent’s permission. Thank you again for taking the time to write to us, and we hope to see you back in the tavern soon!

reviews.review_description: I know yall have said update soon like 5 days ago or smthin like that. Now its 21. where is the update?
I lost all my progress

developer_response.body:
Hi! We’re really sorry about the lost progress, we know how frustrating that feels.

This update includes a huge engine upgrade and a completely new progression system under the hood. That step was critical so we can keep supporting the game on modern tech and so we can at least seriously explore things that many players ask about, like the possibility of co-op in the future. 

Unfortunately, there was no safe way to migrate old saves, and this was a very hard decision for us. The good news is that future updates will not reset progress again.

We also added Sandbox Mode so you can quickly get back to where you were (or even further), with all taverns unlocked and full control over the experience.

We truly hope you’ll give the updated version a chance. Your feedback and support mean the world to us and help us make the game better with every step. Thank you so much for your understanding, your patience, and for being with us on this journey.

reviews.review_description: Soo good but can you guys add multiplayer too it would make it even more popular

developer_response.body:
Hi there! 💛

Thank you so much for the kind words! It really means a lot to us that you love the game!

Multiplayer / co-op is one of the most common requests we get, and it’s definitely something we’d love to explore further. We can’t promise anything yet (it’s a huge technical and design challenge for our tiny team), but the recent big engine and systems update was an important step to even consider features like that in the future.

Thanks again for playing and for the encouragement! 🍻✨

reviews.review_description: The last post you guys gave us of the new leak was in August. I’m honestly worried that you guys just gave up on the game and stopped working on it.

developer_response.body: Hey! That's true, we planned to drop the update earlier, but we had to set up our new company and work through a bunch of formal/legal steps, which slowed things down. But no worries, we haven’t given up at all! The good news: the hard part is behind us, and we’re aiming to release the next update this month (November). We’re finalizing the last bits of approvals and cross-platform scheduling - once that’s done, we’ll lock the date and announce it. Please follow the news in our Discord, we’ll post the release dates there as soon as possible: https://discord.com/invite/ZhUQwEearp. Endless thanks for your patience and for being part of the community! 🍻

reviews.review_description: This is a really great game and if it leaves meta quest Plus I will buy it if that's how good it is very relaxing

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind words! 😊 We’re really happy to hear that you’re enjoying the game and finding it relaxing. Your support means a lot to us, and we’re glad you're having a great time in the tavern. Thanks for playing! 🍻

reviews.review_description:
The controls are a little too clunky, especially with the taps
so far

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback! We’re glad the game looks fun to you. 😊 Could you tell us a bit more about what feels clunky with the controls, especially with the taps? Any extra details would really help us understand the issue better and see what we can improve.

reviews.review_description: YOU should ADD multiplayer THAT would make you BLOW UP

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for playing Innkeeper VR! 🍻

Multiplayer is one of the most requested features, and we’d also love to let people run a tavern together. Right now we’re exploring co-op as a separate mode/map where you and your friends can work in the inn as a team.
That said, proper co-op in VR (with physics, cooking, chopping, NPCs, etc.) is a huge technical challenge for our tiny team, which has only built single-player so far. 

Because of that we want to be honest: we can’t promise multiplayer or give any timeline. Our current focus is stabilizing, polishing, and expanding the single-player game.

If co-op is important to you, we’d love to hear your specific ideas (how many players, more chill vs. more chaotic, what you’d like to do together). You can always drop your feedback in our Discord post about Co-op: https://discord.com/channels/977127742418071602/1442825729267073146. We read everything, even if we can’t reply to every message. 🙏

reviews.review_description: I absolutely love it. There's plenty to do and a good amount of content for an early access quest game. All the mechanics and atmosphere mesh nicely. I really love running my Inn. I hope the devs work on even more content such as even more decor as everything else is perfect for me. It would be great to have multiple inns or multiplayer to have competition for customers. Maybe AI \ LLM staff would be cool so you could chat to them whilst working.... but then again, i might not want to take my headset off again if they did that...

Honestly this game is very underrated, and it just
 deserves more players. It's actually really fun, and I can't wait for the next update!! :D
I recommend this game to new players if you want a relaxing game whenever you’re cutting wood, you don’t need to put it in storage yourself. You can just wait and then it goes to storage. ￼
Its good

It's very fun but I ran into bugs sometimes but there not too bad because I worked around them. Also creators shouldale furniture and stuff stay and not have to replace it everytime you hop on the game. 5 stars

Holy, this game is already good, but here are some recommendations.

FIGHTING WITH THE VIOLENT CUSTOMERS:

You should be able to fight against the violent customers that attack others. It would make the game way more fun. It would also add more of a reason for people to get the game.

HARVESTING:

You should be able to go outside and find and harvest ingredients. This would put creativity at a whole nother level. It'd be fun to do, but it would also be easy for beginners.

UPDATED TUTORIAL:

Make the tutorial set inside the inn, so you know where things are at. Also, explain more on what you are going to do right away after you start your inn.

I watched A Wolf in VR play this and decided I had to try it myself. I absolutely love it and play it sooo much! It's addictive! 

I really enjoy making the food and the drinks and unlocking new recipes. It's fun to experiment but I also like that we can buy recipes. There's a lot more that goes into this game than it seems. And I love that you really have to start from the ground up!

However, I do have the following suggestions. 

1. Teleporting.

2. More holiday or seasonal decorations. Right now there's just Christmas.

3. Decorating rooms makes the price go up.  Right now you're just out the money for the decorations.  It doesn't benefit you at all to decorate them. 

4. The ability to sell back decorations if they are no longer needed or wanted, even at half the price of what you paid. 

5. The ability to modify the colors of the decorations. 

6. More than 3 staff. 

7. Multi-player. 

I realize it's an early access game, so I'm hoping some of these changes occur when it's full version is released.  

When will that be???

I only started playing this game two days ago after watching a wolf in vr play it so i thought id give it ago, the only issue I’ve had so far is in the settings changing the height calibrations, the lowest i can get is 165cm and im 158cm, i leave it at 165cm but my character is bending at the knees and wont stand up properly.

I think it would be great if we could harvest some of the ingredients ourselves. 

Also to be able to have more than 3 staff.

Just got this game, and i love it. The game is so much fun, and it makes me and my family laugh with all the fights.

For the final game i would suggest a multiplayer option! Me and my little brother want to run our own tavern in this game, just a thought! 

Also we need MORE staff options!
The does not update anymore and they do this with most of there games they make in update it a bit and make a new one and work on that and repeat
I've always wanted to try being an Innkeeper and this game really scratches that itch. I desperately need this to be multiplayer though it would be so much fun to split the duties with friends.
Add multiplayer and this game is peak :)

reviews.review_title: Threw half the town in a well
reviews.review_description: I got tired of people starting fights, stealing from me, and sleeping in my bar, so I took extreme measures. Every violator would get dragged by the head and throw into the well. After long enough, the l well had 13 people in it, clipping in and out of each other standing there. I started to realize my bar got barely any visitors and realized what I had done. I had thrown so many people in the well that not many people weren't in it, so I hardly got any business. I had to go and free them all. They were not happy and 3 minutes after freeing them a huge bar fight broke out. 10 out of 10

It's definitely an amazing game for what it is. I have put countless hours into it already and not even done it yet. There is so much more to do than expected from cooking, cleaning, brew making, managing staff, etc etc. The graphics are not top of the line like some games are but for the amount of what this game has to offer already its definitely worth the price and time. This game is definitely a MUST HAVE and its still being developed and worked on including multiplayer update so i am looking forward to seeing the many additions added to this game. Keep up the great work devs.

I would give it 5 stars if there was a multiplayer mode..
WHEN MULTIPLAYER MODE??
WHERE MULTIPLAYER MODE??
WE NEED IT, WE WANT, IT SO PLEASE

Cook, clean, serve, brew, manage, throw out troublemakers by the seat of their pants and make money to upgrade your tavern. You will be plenty busy and immersed here with fun, polished mechanics and lots of upgrades, employees, recipes, decor and other stuff to grind towards. I dont know what exactly possessed me to buy this game, maybe the Youtube vids looked amusing  but first try, I played this until my headset died. Completely lost in this hustle, having a blast  talking smack to customers and forgetting about all the troubles of the real world if only for an hour or two. Well worth the 9 bucks and even in early access there is more love and attentiveness from the developer, who is always updating and adding to this passion project. Bravo!

1. The decorations in the first tavern have no textures 

2. Robbing customers should be faster it is pretty easy to catch them 

3. Add some sort of animal/animals you can purchase as pets that would give you perks

Probably the best game on the market it is very fun to throw out angry customers and making food andd beer. The game also has some length to it you can have up to 3 saves. From chopping wood for fuel to making beer this game has it all. Recommended 10/10

I love the game it's so addicting and it's got so much chaos in it, it's hilarious when I was playing earlier I had half my tavern fighting and like a fifth of them trying to steal from me and then like four people at the front desk for a room it was crazy and then there's the waitress zooming around the tavern and my cleaner is just standing in the back corner watching it all go down like I know they're an NPC but it's just funny them standing in the corner watching everything go down.
-Also idea for the devs multiplayer though I know it's been suggested a lot but it would be so fun. Like just imagine one of you running around trying to cook and the other one trying to serve and just screaming back and forth at each other to get one thing done or the other it'd be fun and chaotic.

First off, i was kinda hesitant at first but after reading reviews, watching some gameplay, i finally bought it today and all i have to say is it’s SO ADDICTIVE. It’s fun as heck, and really what i’ve been looking for in a game!! Only thing is i hope the devs can take a free play mode into consideration, where you have unlimited money to experiment with the ingredients! Even if it doesn’t happen it’s still a super fun game lol

Ideas-

-Other species of customers, elves, dwarfs, etc.

- sick customers, kick them out before it spreads, they are slower. They cough, and they sneeze

- Instead of instantly ordering supplies, you can buy foods and yeast at open markets in the city.(For the regular inn just walk out the front and head to the right side, and for the autumn inn just walk foward into the city) Be careful not to get scammed or get your money stolen by thiefs.

If your not able to add these it's fine the game is great!
Please let my refund I didn’t mean to by it

developer_response.body:
Hey!
Sorry about the accidental purchase, we totally understand. Unfortunately, we don’t have access to billing or refunds from our side - Meta handles these directly.

Please follow this step-by-step guide from Meta: https://www.meta.com/help/quest/828412189021014/. 

Most app refunds are eligible if requested within 14 days and the game was played less than 2 hours. If you don’t see the refund button, please contact Meta Support via the Help Center link above; they can review it for you. 

Once this is sorted out, we’d really appreciate it if you could update your review so it reflects your experience after the refund. Reviews affect our visibility and help other players make informed choices. Thanks for reaching out!

reviews.review_title: Innkeeper:Where My Free Time Went to Die
reviews.review_description: I thought I’d give Innkeeper a quick try before bed. Three hours later, I’m frantically juggling cooking, brewing, and serving drinks to a tavern full of rowdy NPCs while debating if I should expand the menu or just burn the place down. It’s dangerously addicting, like Tetris for hospitality enthusiasts. The gameplay strikes a perfect balance between strategy and chaos, with just enough random disasters (looking at you, "half the tavern breaking into a brawl") to keep things interesting. It’s got that "just one more day" vibe, and I can already see it becoming a favorite. My only complaint? My actual kitchen could use the same attention my virtual tavern is getting.

Hi all, honestly, I bought this game initially just to play around one evening and was surprised how quickly i took to it.  It's very engaging and you quite soon find yourself in bar fights, run out of food or ale, chaos can quickly erupt in your Tavern, and it's fun.

I have already finished the first Tavern and have bought the 5000$ Tavern, decorated it and hired all the staff. It's great to get the cook to cook all the dishes in advance and for her to prepare all the good ale's, Wisky's and Beers. 

Love the new update, and using the Staff properly helps to keep order and the money flowing.

What I would love to also have is a Barman as staff as to free me up to enjoy every aspect of the game. ... Are there plans for this staff member? And a new larger Inn (Tavern) would be great, one where I can have all staff members working and see if I can have the inn running without closing? Possibly 5,6 or 7 staff slots, (for the 5000$ tavern or a bigger Tavern 🫣 maybe two waitresses? 

All in all a great and novel game. 👍

Hope the Devs remain active, and thanks for the game.

Regard, 

A happy inn keeper 😁

50% of the time I cut an ingredient for soups, part of the ingredient just vanishes so I can’t complete a dish… wasting money you don’t have in the beginning of the game, and it only gets worse as you buy more expensive ingredients… fix this because I liked the game.

Also I could not store yeast, since you have to pour it out to interact with it. I placed the whole cup in storage and nothing happened, so I poured it in and wasted ingredients yet again. 

 Just put more storage in the kitchen,   And I don’t mean the treasure chest. More cups for all the yeast to be able to be displayed would just be easier on both us and you.

Please fix your game Dev

reviews.review_title: Super fun really hope for multiplayer
reviews.review_description: Game is super fun easy to advance I love all the different things you can do the only problem I have is sometimes when I reach for another drink I somehow hit the tap on the beer barrel and hear liquid running but by the time I get to the other side of counter it’s empty and I just wasted it all and lost money that might be just a clumsy me thing I don’t know but it’s happened  like four times other than that super fun I do realize it’s in early access so I won’t hold anything against the game I would love to see multiplayer I think that would be super fun

reviews.review_title: I spent the whole day playing this game
reviews.review_description: This game is really fun and addictive.1 thing maybe the devs could add is either multiplayer or people write reviews of your inn and you can read them on your way to improve.
